comments_critique.summary: The discussion touches on the security implications of ECC versus RSA in the context of quantum attacks, a minor comment on proofreading, and a preference for document format. The main substantive comment debates cryptographic choices and their theoretical vulnerabilities.

comments.author: bob1029
comments.text:
> ECC algorithms with smaller key sizes would be more vulnerable to a quantum attack, as it would require a currently theoretical quantum computer with fewer qubits than would be required for an RSA key with the same cryptographic strength [25].

This is what keeps me skeptical about ECC. RSA is really chunky, and maybe that's a fundamental advantage from an information theory perspective. Compromising on the crypto scheme because we can't fit inside UDP seems like a cursed path.

[25]: https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.06752
comments.analysis: This comment provides a nuanced perspective on the relative quantum vulnerability of ECC versus RSA, referencing academic research, and raises a valid concern about cryptographic trade-offs.

comments.author: antonyh
comments.text: I do wish these types of document were published as HTML and not just as PDF.
comments.analysis: This is a reasonable usability suggestion advocating for more accessible document formats, though it does not engage with the technical content.
